Okay, so I told you that there's two biggest pieces to the sale. First one is your mindset, your head game, how excited you are, how much you believe in the product and how much rapport you can build. That's like the front end piece that you have to have your head game right, whatever ritual whatever it is. This is the second piece. If you don't spend enough time here, you haven't created enough of a problem to create enough of a need for a solution. This is the sale. The pain is the pitch. The pitch isn't the pitch. The pain is the pitch because if the pain is high enough, someone's like, I'm going to die tomorrow unless I lose weight. What do you think the likelihood is that they're going to sign up? Really, really high. So what your objective is to increase their awareness of their pain, all right, and then increase their awareness of why they failed in the past so that you can set up the solution that we're going to give them to solve it, right. So what questions do you ask? And this is just a series of questions that you're going to ask in a loop until you've exhausted all of the things. All right, so first thing is, well, how long you been trying to get this result, right, how long you been trying to lose weight? Like, uh, I don't know. I haven't really been trying. Like, awesome, how long have you wanted to lose weight? And this is a really deep one, right? How long have you wanted to lose weight my whole life? Like, okay, so you haven't been trying or someone like I've been trying for three months. I'm like, okay, so you've been trying for three months. You've wanted to lose weight your whole life, you've been trying for the last three months. Great. How much weight have you lost? Now, there's two ways this can go. All right, now let's say they have five things they've done. Four of them didn't work. One of them did. No matter what they say, it always works for us because if they say something that didn't work, so these four things, which most of the time is what it is because that's why they're there. All right. So like, you always have the upper hand in it because they walked in the door, which means they have a problem, right? If it's like someone's like, why are you have personal strength? I'm like, well, clearly it's not doing his job because you're here, right? So how long have you been trying to achieve the result? How long have you wanted to achieve the result? Okay. And so what have you done? So you said you did weight watchers. Okay, awesome. How'd that work for you? Then they tell you, you know, I lost 30 pounds. Awesome. So then what happened? Have you kept it off? No, I gained 40 pounds back afterwards. Got it. Why do you think that happened? Okay. Now, what we're going to be working on here is that we have the three main pieces, right? We have fitness, nutrition, and accountability. And so what you're looking for and all the things that they're going to tell you is what elements of those things they have or what things they didn't have. So wait, and the more you do this, the more you've identified them, you're not going to need to think about it. It'll just come like what I mean is like weight watchers, that's nutrition and accountability. They don't have fitness, right? Which means that they're going to lose weight, but they're going to lose muscle and fat because they're not doing any fitness training, especially not any high intensity or resistance training, which means they're losing muscle, which sets them up for regain on the back end. So you're like, so why don't you think you kept the weight off? And they're like, I don't know. As soon as I was like, I went off the diet, I ballooned back up. I'm like, you know, you know, muscle burns fat, right? And they're like, yeah. I'm like, so in that example, like you had, you had fitness, right? So you had nutrition, right? They helped me with the food. That was really important. Like, yeah, that was really, that was really helpful for me. Awesome. And did you like people weighing you and everything? Like, yeah, that was super like it was nice to be accountable. So you're going to take the good and we're going to say how it's similar to our program. You're going to save it for later, but you're, you're highlighting those things. You're basically telling them the important information of the things they just told you and letting them just, and just disregarding the rest, right? But you didn't have that fitness. You didn't have that muscle, right? And so that's what happened. So when you lost weight, did you look exactly the way you wanted? Or were you like, man, I'm still not tight. Most of the times are like, yeah, I still wasn't really as tight as I would have wanted. I was smaller, but I wasn't tall. And we're like, yeah, exactly. We need that fitness piece, right? The next thing I might say is, you know, I signed up for 24 hour fitness. I've been a member for five years. I've never gone or I went once or twice. And, you know, I, you know, I didn't lose any weight, right? So there you had fitness, barely, you know, they would give you access to facility. They didn't tell you what to do. They didn't train you there. They didn't give you a workout. They didn't make sure you're actually pushing yourself and progressing, right? And they're like, no, of course not. And forget meal plan, right? They didn't tell you what to eat. Like, no, God, no. And I'm guessing no one put like, they only call you if your credit card declines, right? Yeah, haha, right? So they were 0 for three, which no, like, no shit. That's why you didn't lose any weight, right? You had zero of the three main components, right? So what else have you done? And then they'll tell you the next thing. You're like, awesome. So you had one of the three main components, two of the three main components. So if it's something that failed, it's usually have one or zero of the components that are necessary for weight loss. If they have two of the components, usually any kind of diet program or diet and accountability program, they're going to lose weight. A lot of times they don't keep it off because they didn't learn how to do it. They just followed a circuit and then after that, they went back off, right? And if it's diet only, they're going to lose muscle, right? And so those are the main pieces that we're going to use when we're coming back at the end. Now, once you've exhausted that loop of everything that they've done in the past, now sometimes people are going to be like, I've only done one thing. I'm like, I haven't tried shit. They're like, okay, then what's been the issue? You said you wanted to lose weight for 10 years. You tried or you haven't tried yet. So then what's been the main problem? All right. Now this is the bold piece on the sheet. You'll see this is the main nut. This is the main thing is that you need to get them to say, it's usually a combination of I need, I just need to be motivated. I'm lazy. I just, I need someone to jumpstart me, whatever it is, you need them to say something about motivation and accountability. And then usually something about knowledge. Like I didn't know. So some people are like, I know what to do. I just don't do it. Or I don't know what to do and I don't do it. Some people are like, I'm working out five days a week and I'm not losing weight. So they're doing it, but they just need the right knowledge. And so whatever it is, it's usually a combination of those two things, which is perfect because we're going to provide both. And if they have failed up to this point, because they haven't had those things. That makes sense. Okay. So once they've said those, once they've said, you know, I'm just not that motivated. I'm like, got it. Like now it's like, I'm finished the sequence of bringing the pain. I'm going to reiterate all the pains that I remind you of everything you just said. So just make sure I got everything. So you went to 24 fitness, you've been a member for five years, you've been a few times, you get motivated for a couple of weeks, but you don't see any results. You go there for, you know, hop on the treadmill. You said you're not, you're like not really experienced with it. You're not like a personal trainer, right? They're like, no, not like, right. So, you know, you don't make your own workouts. You're just kind of going with the flow, right? They're like, yeah, got it. So that's what you did. You haven't lost any weight from that. Got it. Weight washers, you said you lost 30 pounds, but then you gained back 40. So you're actually up 10. So that's not, that didn't really work for you. You actually gained weight from that whole thing, like net. Like, yeah, I guess so. Like that sucks. I'm sorry. Um, now, and then besides that, you, um, try to weight loss pill, got super jittery and didn't lose anything. Right. Is that about right? Okay. And then in the last three months, you put on 10 more pounds and you're like, that is it. I need to do something right now. They're like, yeah, that's about it. And so right now the biggest problem has been you're not motivated or, you know, you need someone to hold you accountable and need someone to get you jump started and, uh, you need the right plan. That sound about right? And they'll be like, yes, like awesome. So, uh, I think you're going to like, think you're going to like the program that we have for you. Okay. So that's the transition to the pitch. Okay. So you brought the pain and then you just transition. It's one or two sentences. It's just awesome. So you want to hear about this program and then they'll invite you. And they'll say, yeah, tell me about it. Right. Cause they're here. You've spent all this time talking to them, listening to them. And before we get to the actual pitch, I just want to bring up this one point. People pay people to listen to them. It's a, it's very valuable for people to have people listen. They pay therapists hundreds of dollars an hour to just listen to them. Okay. So sales is about listening and it's really about asking the right questions to lead them down the thought path that becomes, that makes them more aware of their problem and then eventually more aware of the solution and how hopefully if we do it right, that we're the solution that they're looking for. Okay. So just, just this reiteration, like it's, it's really like you just got to listen. And if you feel like, if you feel like they don't like you before you get to the pitch, they're probably not going to buy. So just spend more time, just get to know them better, build more rapport. And sometimes you'll be surprised to be building more rapport. All of a sudden they'll remember things that they did. All right. Some people come in just having a bad day and you just got to be there a good day. All right. Cool. All right. So here's the pitch.
